title: Sannantha tozerensis
intro: Sannantha tozerensis, also known as Tozer's sannantha, is a rare and endangered shrub native to the tropical rainforests of Australia. This plant has been traditionally used by indigenous communities to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Sannantha tozerensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, cytotoxic, and immunomodulatory activities. Herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, sores, and skin conditions.
